entries.rationale: Preserve reviewed behavior and lessons while replacing historically coupled structure.

entries.component: protocol discoveries and physical evidence claims
entries.rationale: Claims may be admitted only with provenance, privacy classification, and evidence links; raw captures remain outside the product repository.
Retain proven behavior, UAPI lessons, and failure tests while rebuilding a minimal generation-owned transport boundary.
Replace profile-combination coupling and presentation leakage with typed state machines, composable profiles, leases, and bounded actors.
Split receiver, child, route, capability, evidence, and upstream presentation metadata into canonical composable registries.
Reuse OpenRGB-owned model presentation and effects through a stable SDK while retaining HyperFlux as the only receiver transport.
Transform licensed upstream metadata reproducibly; isolate optional D-Bus compatibility from the official daemon and core architecture.
Build a native SDK backend after resource ownership exists; do not monkeypatch or suppress unrelated devices.
Convert durable failures and evidence into deterministic replay; keep mission-specific and live sender machinery in the laboratory.
Preserve ordinary-user lifecycle findings while moving decisions into tested typed utilities and one central error catalog.
Generate live product truth from registries while retaining audience-specific authored guidance and no engineering mission IDs in product copy.

entries.component: license text and project-owned licensing decision
entries.rationale: GPL-2.0-only is compatible with the kernel and OpenRGB boundaries; third-party material remains independently reviewed.

entries.component: raw captures, private identifiers, Stage-A operations, and arbitrary live senders
entries.rationale: These remain linked research evidence and can never become product dependencies or public support artifacts.
